title: Add --all-tags to pull command
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Add --all-tags for the `podman pull` command so all tags
of an image will be pulled, not just ':latest'.  Emulates
the change in Buildah https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1263

FWIW the bash completions for all-tags were already in place.

=========
Test Results:

comments.body: @rhatdan, @baude or @mheon, can one of you ask for this to be retested please?  I tried but the bot ignored me.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2196) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2274) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
FWIW, I think I finally figured out the test issues that had been plaguing me, the one failure now appears to be a flake to me, unrelated code.
Oh my goodness.  Finally green test buttons on this one.  @mheon, @rhatdan, @baude, PTAL

I agree, @TomSweeneyRedHat Open an issue to fix this in the future, but lets merge to get this into podman 1.1.
/lgtm
